metadata.archetype_justification: The application owns persistent state (SQLite/PostgreSQL databases storing artists, albums, tracks, history, user config) and exposes full CRUD operations on these business entities via a REST API. Classified as stateful-crud.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute — application distributes as self-hosted platform packages with no cloud compute infrastructure
top_gaps.impact: Blocks containerization, auto-scaling, and cloud-native deployment

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code
top_gaps.gap: No IaC exists — all infrastructure is manually provisioned by end users
top_gaps.impact: Prevents reproducible deployments, environment consistency, and automated DR

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: Tightly-coupled monolith with shared database, extensive cross-module dependencies
top_gaps.impact: Limits independent scaling, deployment velocity, and team autonomy

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No distributed tracing or OpenTelemetry instrumentation
top_gaps.impact: Cannot trace request flows or diagnose performance issues in production

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or cloud audit logging infrastructure
top_gaps.impact: No compliance-grade audit trail for API actions or system changes

quick_agent_wins.name: API-aware Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: OpenAPI 3.0.4 specification available at src/Lidarr.Api.V1/openapi.json with comprehensive endpoint documentation
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that can discover and invoke Lidarr's REST API endpoints as tools — managing artists, albums, searches, downloads, and system operations programmatically
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Agent integration would require exposing the spec at a discoverable endpoint and implementing proper API key auth delegation

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists — README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md, extensive wiki, and 80+ database migration files documenting schema evolution
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent that indexes Lidarr's documentation and code to answer developer questions about the codebase, API usage, and troubleshooting
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Would need to index external wiki content (wiki.servarr.com) alongside repo docs

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Given that Lidarr is a self-hosted desktop/server application with P2 priority, containerize first then selectively extract high-value bounded contexts

findings.title: No Managed Compute
findings.description: All compute is self-managed by end users. The application distributes as platform-specific packages for 11 runtime identifiers with no managed container orchestration or serverless compute.
findings.gap: No managed compute — application has no cloud deployment model.
findings.recommendation: Containerize the application using .NET 8 runtime and deploy to EKS with Fargate.

findings.title: No Managed Databases
findings.description: Databases are entirely self-managed. Default is embedded SQLite; PostgreSQL is optional and user-provisioned. No managed database service configured.
findings.gap: All databases self-managed with no automated failover, backups, or scaling.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL. Application already has full PostgreSQL support.

findings.title: No Workflow Orchestration
findings.description: Multi-step workflows (search-download-import-organize) are entirely hardcoded in application logic with no dedicated orchestration service.
findings.gap: Business-critical multi-step workflows have no orchestration primitives, retry logic, or visual management.
findings.recommendation: Extract the download-import-organize pipeline into AWS Step Functions for retry logic and visibility.

findings.title: In-Process Messaging Only
findings.description: Application uses an in-process event aggregator (IEventAggregator/IHandle<T>) for pub/sub but no distributed messaging infrastructure.
findings.gap: No managed messaging for cross-service communication when decomposition occurs.
findings.recommendation: Introduce EventBridge for cross-service event propagation when extracting services.

findings.title: No Network Security Infrastructure
findings.description: No VPC, subnets, security groups, or network segmentation. Application is self-hosted with direct network exposure.
findings.gap: No cloud network security infrastructure whatsoever.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC with private subnets, security groups, and VPC endpoints when deploying to AWS.

findings.title: No API Entry Point
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront. Application exposes Kestrel directly.
findings.gap: Services exposed directly with no gateway or load balancer.
findings.recommendation: Deploy behind API Gateway with throttling, auth, and request validation.

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration. Application runs as single instance.
findings.gap: All capacity statically provisioned with no ability to respond to demand changes.
findings.recommendation: Configure HPA on EKS with custom metrics when containerized.

findings.title: No Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No IaC exists — no Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm, or Kustomize files in the repository.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. No infrastructure defined in code.
findings.recommendation: Create IaC (CDK preferred for .NET ecosystem) defining VPC, EKS, Aurora, ECR, and operational resources.

findings.title: CI Automation Without CD Pipeline
findings.description: Azure DevOps Pipelines provides comprehensive CI (build, test, analyze) but no deployment pipeline exists.
findings.gap: Strong CI but no CD — builds produce artifacts but do not deploy to any environment.
findings.recommendation: Extend pipeline with deploy stages to EKS using staged deployment (dev → staging → production).

findings.title: Tightly-Coupled Monolith
findings.description: Single deployable unit with 2,300+ C# files in one core project, shared database with 80 migrations, and pervasive cross-module dependencies.
findings.gap: No independently deployable components, shared mutable state, single database.
findings.recommendation: Containerize as-is, then apply conditional/adaptive decomposition to extract high-value bounded contexts.

findings.title: Primarily Synchronous Communication
findings.description: Internal method calls are synchronous. In-process event aggregator provides pub/sub but is not distributed.
findings.gap: Primarily synchronous with only in-process pub/sub. Background processing lacks distributed async patterns.
findings.recommendation: Convert IHandle<T> events to distributed async messaging via EventBridge when decomposing.

findings.title: Partial Long-Running Process Handling
findings.description: Internal command queue with status polling exists but runs in-process with no distributed job infrastructure.
findings.gap: Job processing is in-process. Large operations can still block without dedicated async infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Migrate command queue to SQS with Step Functions for multi-step operations.

findings.title: API Versioning Prefix Only
findings.description: URL path versioning (/api/v1/) applied consistently but only one version exists with no backward compatibility policy.
findings.gap: Single version prefix with no versioning strategy, deprecation policy, or migration path.
findings.recommendation: Formalize versioning strategy with backward compatibility policy and plan for v2.

findings.title: Configuration-Based Service Endpoints
findings.description: External service endpoints configured via environment variables and database settings. No dynamic service discovery.
findings.gap: No dynamic discovery; endpoints managed through static configuration.
findings.recommendation: Implement AWS Cloud Map or Kubernetes DNS for service discovery in multi-service deployment.

findings.title: Local Filesystem for Unstructured Data
findings.description: Album artwork, media covers, and metadata stored on local filesystem with no managed object storage.
findings.gap: All unstructured data on local file systems, inaccessible for cloud-native workloads.
findings.recommendation: Migrate media cover storage to S3 with CloudFront CDN for serving.

findings.title: Database Version Not Pinned in IaC
findings.description: No IaC defines database versions. CI tests against PostgreSQL 14/15 but production version depends on user deployment.
findings.gap: No version pinning in IaC. EOL status unknown for user-deployed instances.
findings.recommendation: Pin Aurora PostgreSQL engine version in IaC. Establish version update procedure.

findings.title: No Audit Logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or cloud audit logging. Application logs via NLog are not immutable audit trails.
findings.gap: No immutable audit logging infrastructure.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock.

findings.title: Static API Key Authentication
findings.description: API uses static API key with no token-based auth, no expiry, no scoped permissions.
findings.gap: No OAuth2/JWT; static key with no rotation or per-user attribution.
findings.recommendation: Implement OAuth2/JWT via Amazon Cognito or API Gateway authorizers.

findings.title: No Centralized Identity Integration
findings.description: Self-managed authentication with local user store. No external IdP, no OIDC/SAML, no SSO.
findings.gap: Entirely self-managed auth with no external identity provider integration.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito for centralized identity and SSO support.

findings.title: No Secrets Management System
findings.description: Credentials in environment variables without encryption or rotation. No Secrets Manager or Vault.
findings.gap: No dedicated secrets management. No rotation configured.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with AWS Secrets Manager with automated rotation for database credentials.

findings.title: Limited Compute Hardening
findings.description: CI uses current base images. Dependabot covers devcontainers only. No vulnerability scanning for deployed instances.
findings.gap: No vulnerability scanning for NuGet/npm packages. Dependabot scope too limited.
findings.recommendation: Extend Dependabot to NuGet/npm. Add container scanning when Dockerfiles are created. Use Bottlerocket for EKS nodes.

findings.title: Partial Security Pipeline
findings.description: SonarCloud integrated for code quality but is not a dedicated security scanner. No SAST, no dependency vulnerability scanning in pipeline.
findings.gap: No dedicated SAST tool. No dependency vulnerability scanning for NuGet/npm.
findings.recommendation: Add CodeQL/Semgrep for SAST. Add dotnet list package --vulnerable and yarn audit to pipeline.

findings.title: No Distributed Tracing
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, or trace ID propagation. Only NLog text-based logging.
findings.gap: Cannot trace request flows or diagnose cross-service performance issues.
findings.recommendation: Instrument with OpenTelemetry SDK for .NET and export to X-Ray.

findings.title: No SLO Definitions
findings.description: No SLOs defined. Internal health checks monitor readiness but not service levels.
findings.gap: No formal service level definitions or error budget tracking.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for API latency, search availability, download success rate.

findings.title: No Business Metrics
findings.description: No custom metrics for business outcomes. Only basic telemetry opt-in exists.
findings.gap: No visibility into application performance from a user-outcome perspective.
findings.recommendation: Publish key metrics (albums imported/day, download success rate) to CloudWatch.

findings.title: No Anomaly Detection or Alerting
findings.description: No external alerting. Health checks visible only in the application UI.
findings.gap: No CloudWatch alarms, no anomaly detection, no external monitoring.
findings.recommendation: Implement CloudWatch alarms on error rates and latency with SNS alerting.

findings.title: No Deployment Strategy
findings.description: Self-update mechanism replaces running binary directly. No blue/green, canary, or rolling deployments.
findings.gap: Direct binary replacement with no staged rollout or rollback capability.
findings.recommendation: Implement canary deployments via ArgoCD Rollouts on EKS.

findings.title: Integration Tests Without Cloud Validation
findings.description: Integration tests exist and run in CI against self-contained app, but do not test cloud deployment.
findings.gap: Tests validate local operation only. No cloud integration validation.
findings.recommendation: Add integration test stages validating against deployed cloud infrastructure.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, no SSM automation, no self-healing beyond log DB auto-recreation.
findings.gap: Incident response is entirely ad hoc with no automation.
findings.recommendation: Create operational runbooks and SSM Automation documents for common failures.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership
findings.description: No dashboards, alarm owners, or team attribution for monitoring.
findings.gap: No observability ownership structure exists.
findings.recommendation: Establish CloudWatch dashboards per functional area with defined alarm owners.

findings.title: No Resource Tagging
findings.description: No AWS resources exist, therefore no tagging. Governance must be established from day one.
findings.gap: No tagging governance established for when cloud resources are created.
findings.recommendation: Implement mandatory tagging (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter) in IaC from inception.

immediate_actions: Extract notification service as first microservice

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 and no container definitions found in repository

triggering_questions.note: No managed compute; no Dockerfile exists

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures); databases already open source (SQLite, PostgreSQL)

triggering_questions.note: No stored procedures — trigger threshold not met

pathways.not_triggered_reason: All database engines in use (SQLite, PostgreSQL) are already open source. No commercial engines detected.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=1 (self-managed databases), DATA-Q3=2 (versions not pinned in IaC)

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads detected. Application is a media manager, not analytics system.

triggering_questions.note: Score < 3 but contextual guard blocks — no data processing workloads exist

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No streaming, ETL, data pipeline, or analytics artifacts found. Application manages media collections, not data processing.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q10=1 (no IaC), OPS-Q5=1 (no deployment strategy), OPS-Q6=2 (limited integration testing)

triggering_questions.note: Integration tests exist but not for cloud deployment

immediate_actions: Add deploy stage to CI pipeline

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context describes 'Music collection manager' with no AI-related signal terms.
